values: Reusable release guide for development validation, production readiness, and post-deploy smoke testing
Use a dedicated test account, test email, Stripe test mode, and orders prefixed with QA.
Complete the Release Checklist first. Stop if any P0 gate fails.
Filter Test Cases by area, priority, environment, or device. Record Pass/Fail and evidence.
Use Device Matrix to confirm desktop, iPhone, Android, and tablet coverage.
Create a Defect Log row for each failure and link its test case ID.
Approve only when all P0/P1 tests pass or have an explicitly accepted exception.
All P0 and P1 cases Pass, or an owner documents and accepts the exception.
Zero open Critical or High defects. Medium defects require an owner and target date.
Use Stripe test mode on development. Never place an unapproved live production charge.
Do deep testing on development. Production receives a controlled smoke test after deployment.
Use test customers/orders and clean them up. Do not alter real customer orders for QA.
Attach screenshot, order number, deployment URL, console log, or defect ID for every failure.

values: Filter by priority, area, environment, or device. Enter execution results in the white columns.
Compare main and developement commit history; document intentional differences.
No unexpected divergence; production changes are understood.
Run git status and review every tracked/untracked file.
Only intended release files are present; no secrets, temp files, or accidental outputs.
Confirm Production tracks main and Preview/dev.shrish.co tracks developement.
Production cannot deploy from an unintended branch.
Open dev.shrish.co and inspect deployment status and browser console.
Preview is Ready, correct commit is served, no boot errors.
Confirm functions/package.json uses supported Node runtime and dependencies install cleanly.
Functions use Node 22 (or current approved runtime) and build successfully.
Verify intended Cloud Functions and Firestore rules are deployed to the correct project.
Deployed versions match the release commit.
Check required Stripe, Resend, Maps, PostHog, URLs, tax, and shipping variables without exposing values.
Every required variable exists in the intended environment and no secret is client-exposed.
Verify webhook endpoint, selected events, signing secret, and recent successful deliveries.
Webhook deliveries return 2xx and use the correct environment secret.
Confirm STRIPE_PAYMENTS_ENABLED and test/live key modes match release intent.
No accidental live charging in dev; production behavior matches approved release.
Record the last known-good commit and Vercel deployment before release.
Rollback can be completed quickly without guessing.
Hard reload; inspect CSS/JS/image responses and service-worker version.
New assets are served; old cached behavior does not persist.
Open home, shop, checkout, account, legal pages; add one item without completing live payment.
Critical production paths load and behave normally.
Open Home, About, Shop, Recipes, Contact, Account, Privacy, Refund, Terms.
Each page returns content, has no blank state, and shows the shared header/footer.
Review logo, nav links, search, account, logout, and cart across widths.
Header remains aligned with no clipping or unintended wrapping.
Review logo, cart, and hamburger at 360, 375, 390, 412, and 430px.
Controls are evenly aligned and do not overlap content.
Open menu, use close/hamburger, tap outside, press Escape where available.
Menu opens once, closes reliably, and restores page scrolling.
Check Home, About, Shop, Recipes, Contact, Cart, Search, Order Now, Account/Logout.
Items are readable, tappable, correctly ordered, and route correctly.
Search a full product name, partial name, and no-match term.
Relevant shop results appear; no-match state is helpful and reversible.
Add, increment, decrement, remove products; navigate between pages.
Header and floating cart counts always equal cart quantity.
Review shipping/mango message for wrapping, accuracy, and link behavior.
Message is readable, current, and does not push or cover the header.
Open all legal, contact, WhatsApp, Instagram, and policy links.
Links are correct, safe, and do not produce 404s.
Use browser back/forward repeatedly after filters and modal use.
Expected page state is restored without stale overlays or errors.
Open homepage and observe first slide.
Pickles are the entrance slide with matching image, copy, and CTA.
Slides progress Pickles, Podi, Mangoes, Tathi Thandra in approved order.
Each slide remains visible about 6 seconds and is readable.
Observe every slide image, eyebrow, heading, body, and buttons.
Image and text always describe the same product group.
Click/tap previous and next several times.
Controls move exactly one slide and wrap correctly.
Select each dot and observe active state.
Correct slide opens and active indicator updates.
Hover/focus or interact with slide controls and CTA.
Autoplay does not steal the slide while customer is interacting.
Shop opens with the intended Pickle, Podi, Mango, or Tathi Thandra context.
Review images and text at standard viewport sizes.
Product remains inspectable; headline/buttons fit; next content is hinted without overlap.
Open chat and scroll through hero and first section.
Chat and cart controls never cover hero CTAs, arrows, or important text.
Review filters for All Products, Sweets, Pickles, Podi, Snacks, Mangoes and veg/non-veg subfilters where applicable.
Categories are logically grouped, aligned, and easy to scan.
Compare displayed counts with visible products after each filter.
Counts equal the number of matching catalog products.
Select All Products after using another filter.
All eligible products return and active state updates.
Select Sweets and any available subfilters.
Only sweets display; unrelated products are excluded.
Only non-vegetarian pickles display with preorder messaging where applicable.
Only snacks display and zero-state is accurate if none are live.
Only mango products display with correct availability.
Place pointer over category sidebar and use mouse wheel.
The page scrolls normally; the sidebar does not trap scrolling.
Set minimum/maximum boundaries and clear them.
Only products in range display and clearing restores results.
Test Featured, price low/high, and name sorting if available.
Ordering is correct and active filters remain applied.
Search English and alternate product names such as podi/powder or prawn/shrimp.
Expected products are discoverable and result count is correct.
Review card density at 1366, 1440, and 1920 widths.
Cards are about 30% more compact while text, prices, and buttons remain readable.
Scroll several card types, including long names and multiple variants.
No text/button overlap, horizontal scroll, or clipped price.
Inspect Available, Preorder, Coming Soon, and unavailable states.
Badge, button, and actual purchasability agree.
Request notification with valid and invalid email; repeat request.
Valid request is saved once; duplicate and invalid submissions are handled clearly.
Open Details on products across categories.
Correct modal opens with title, description, pricing, variants, ingredients, policies, and close controls.
Open modal, click each thumbnail, use keyboard/touch controls.
Every expected image loads, switches correctly, and stays within the frame.
Open card/modal for a product with no approved photo.
SHRISH logo is used; no AI/stock/old mismatched image appears.
Add each size/price option and inspect cart.
Correct variant, unit price, name, and quantity are stored.
Add from card, add again, then open cart.
Cart updates once per click and merges/increments the matching variant correctly.
Product cannot be added; Notify action is offered instead.
Hard reload shop and find the edited item.
Approved price/content/image/availability changes are reflected without unrelated changes.
Cart contents, variants, and quantities persist correctly.
Quantity and totals update exactly once per tap/click.
Quantity decreases by one and totals recalculate.
Centered SHRISH-themed confirmation asks whether to remove the item.
Choose Cancel and press Escape where supported.
Dialog closes and item remains unchanged.
Only the selected line is removed and totals/counts update.
Inspect delete control on desktop and mobile.
Boxed trash icon is visible, labeled for accessibility, and does not overlap price/quantity.
Activate trash icon, cancel once, confirm once.
The same themed confirmation is used and the correct item is removed.
No stale totals remain; customer can return to shop.
Compare line totals, subtotal, tax, shipping, discount, and grand total.
Math is correct to cents and labels are unambiguous.
Review order card, quantity, price, delete, forms, and sticky CTA while scrolling.
No overlaps, clipped fields, hidden actions, or horizontal scrolling.
Scroll from top to bottom and open/close menu/dialog.
CTA stays usable without covering required fields, dialogs, or browser safe area.
Submit blank name, phone, and email; then correct each field.
Inline errors identify missing/invalid data and clear when corrected.
Try short, formatted US, and international-style values.
Accepted formats are intentional; impossible values are blocked.
Malformed email is blocked; valid email is accepted.
Enter punctuation and a long but reasonable note.
Text is retained, safely displayed, and stored with order.
Double-click/tap submit and simulate slow response.
One order/payment session is created and button shows progress.
Open checkout and inspect fulfillment/payment options.
Pay at pickup is available; shipping and Stripe-only requirement are not shown.
Pay at pickup is available and shipping is unavailable for the mixed cart.
Online Stripe payment is required; Pay at pickup is not offered.
Remove all mango items without reloading.
Checkout immediately switches to non-mango online-payment rules.
Return to shop, add mango, reopen checkout.
Checkout follows mixed-cart pickup rule consistently.
Approved pickup locations display and one is required.
Pickup location cards and pickup-only instructions are hidden.
Review recipient, address line, city, state, ZIP, optional unit.
Required shipping fields are visible, labeled, and validate correctly.
Type a partial US street address slowly.
Relevant address suggestions appear without exposing the key beyond approved browser use.
Street, city, state, ZIP, and country populate accurately and remain editable.
Checkout remains usable and validates required fields.
Type an address and inspect error handling.
No crash; customer sees a non-blocking fallback path.
Calculate expected tax using configured rate and compare checkout/Stripe.
Displayed, saved, and charged tax match to the cent.
Configured standard shipping (currently $8.99 unless changed) is added once.
Free shipping starts at the configured threshold with clear messaging.
Attempt to reach shipping through UI and crafted state.
Shipping is blocked for mango-containing carts.
Complete checkout with Stripe success test card.
One successful payment, one order, paid status, and confirmation page result.
Clear recoverable error; no paid order and cart remains available.
Use authentication-required test card and complete/cancel challenge.
Both paths return safely with accurate order/payment state.
Customer returns to the same environment with cart/order state intact.
Success/cancel returns to dev.shrish.co, never shrish.co.
Review confirmation page and account prompt.
Order number is prominent, gold, readable, and consistent everywhere.
Inspect webhook delivery and stored order after completion.
Webhook is verified and order becomes paid exactly once.
No duplicate order, email, inventory update, or accounting entry.
Test environment and known product price
Alter client/cart price before creating checkout session.
Server ignores/rejects tampered price and Stripe uses authoritative catalog price.
Apply valid code, wrong case, then remove it.
Discount, tax, total, and usage rules calculate correctly.
Apply below and above discount value.
Discount never makes merchandise total negative and totals remain accurate.
Apply code before and after choosing shipping.
Shipping becomes free only when eligibility requirements are met.
Test each restriction and repeat one-use code.
Invalid uses are rejected with specific, safe messages.
Register with valid details and verify account state.
Account is created, signed in, and profile is available.
Try duplicate email, weak password, mismatch, and missing fields.
Specific errors display without exposing sensitive details.
Sign in, navigate, refresh, then sign out.
Session persists as intended and sign-out removes protected access.
One safe reset flow is sent and UI does not reveal unknown accounts.
Update name, phone, preferred pickup, city/address.
Changes save and prefill checkout without corrupting order history.
Guest order and matching account data
Eligible order links once to the correct account.
Open history and compare order cards to Firestore/admin.
Order number, date, items, total, payment, status, and location match.
Open and close details; review all values.
Correct order opens and modal is responsive/keyboard accessible.
Order totals and admin view update safely.
Use available edit/shop path to add a new product if supported.
New item can be added only when business rules allow, with correct total/payment handling.
Order is canceled once and cannot be processed as active.
Attempt edit/cancel through UI and crafted request.
Server and UI reject unauthorized state changes.
Choose Order Again and inspect cart.
Available items/variants are added; unavailable products are clearly handled.
Submit rating and answers; repeat submission.
Feedback saves once and appears in admin with correct order/customer.
Attempt to access another user's order/profile identifiers.
Unauthorized reads/writes are denied by UI and Firestore rules.
Submit notify request and inspect admin/Firestore.
Request contains correct product and contact data once.
Two users subscribed to different products
Mark one product available through the approved admin action.
Only subscribers to that product receive the availability email.
Use price sync without changing availability.
No availability email is sent and no product is toggled live.
One correctly branded reminder references the right order/location.
Correct number and prefilled, accurate order message are used.
Complete pickup and Stripe test orders
Order number, items, totals, payment, and fulfillment details are accurate.
Order remains valid; error is logged and admin/customer receives safe feedback.
Attempt admin URL and sign in with each account.
Only authorized admins can view or mutate admin data.
Open every sidebar section and return to Orders.
All sections render, active state is correct, no content is hidden by navigation.
Reconcile total orders, pending, fulfilled, revenue, boxes, products live, subscribers.
Cards match source data and definitions.
Search/filter by order, customer, location, date, and status.
Only matching active orders display and counts recalculate.
Sum product quantities manually and compare chips.
Order, box, and item counts are accurate and readable.
Create a manual pickup order with multiple items.
One valid order appears in active/admin/account flows with correct totals.
Edit customer/items/quantities where allowed and save.
Changes persist safely and totals/counts update.
Fulfill one order and inspect processed/all/accounting.
Order leaves active list and is fulfilled exactly once.
Select a subset and use bulk fulfill.
Only selected/filtered eligible orders change state.
Apply each action and confirm resulting lists/statuses.
Transitions follow allowed state rules and are reversible only where intended.
All states display in the correct section with complete data.
Rows/columns match filtered orders and values are usable.
Payment type checkboxes are present; How to Use and Revenue are absent; layout is legible.
Unneeded Payment and Status columns remain removed where requested without losing actions.
Confirm form is hidden; open Add New Product, complete, save, cancel.
Form appears only on request and saves/cancels predictably.
Filter each category and scroll cards.
Filters align, product cards stay compact, controls remain readable.
Change one safe field, save, reload admin and shop.
Only intended field changes and storefront reflects it.
Toggle live, save, reload shop; toggle off again.
Only target product changes availability and notify workflow runs only when appropriate.
Mango products off and mixed catalog
Record availability; run Sync Prices to Live Store; reload admin/shop.
Prices sync as designed; availability flags, especially mangoes, do not change.
Only intended product ordering changes in storefront.
Hidden item is not purchasable/discoverable; unhide restores it.
Review primary/gallery path fields and save approved images.
Valid paths persist, render, and do not overwrite unrelated galleries.
Search/export; inspect orders; test permitted deletion.
Counts are accurate; only accounts with no orders can be deleted.
Filter/search by rating/location/product and inspect summary.
Summary and rows match submissions and remain readable.
Data is accurate and deletion targets only selected record.
Create/edit/disable percent, fixed, free shipping, minimum, cap, expiry, one-use codes.
Rules persist and checkout enforces exactly what admin configured.
Inspect refund eligibility and test-mode refund flow.
Customer refund UI remains hidden; admin refund is deliberate, auditable, and updates status.
Use dropdowns to select open/closed batches and open breakdown.
No long uncontrolled scroll; selected batch metrics are compact and correct.
Save tally, close, locate closed, reopen, verify totals.
State and totals persist without duplicate batches.
Export CSV and reconcile key fields.
Export matches displayed batch and is usable for bookkeeping.
Review invoice, cash, Zelle, card, and balance inputs.
Panels are compact, aligned, and all numeric calculations are accurate.
Open Growth Dashboard and compare key events/metrics with source.
Charts/metrics load without exposing PII and definitions are clear.
Inspect title for every indexable page.
Each title is unique, descriptive, and includes SHRISH appropriately.
Inspect description tags for indexable pages.
Descriptions are unique, accurate, and compelling without keyword stuffing.
Inspect canonical on Home, Shop, About, Contact, Recipes, Privacy, Refund, Terms.
Each points to its preferred www.shrish.co URL; no duplicate/missing canonical.
Public content is indexable; order, account, admin, and development are protected from indexing.
Open sitemap and verify listed URLs, protocol, host, and current public pages.
Only canonical public URLs are listed and all return 200.
Run Google rich result/schema validation on organization and product data.
Valid schema uses accurate business/product information and prices/availability.
Inspect og:title, description, URL, image and share preview.
Share preview is branded, readable, and uses an approved image.
Inspect H1-H3 hierarchy and visible page names.
One meaningful H1 per page and logical nested headings.
Inspect product, hero, recipe, and logo alt text.
Alt text identifies meaningful images; decorative images are appropriately ignored.
Crawl internal links and inspect network 404/500 responses.
No broken internal links, missing scripts/styles, or missing product images.
Open Privacy, Refund, Terms from footer and checkout agreement.
Pages load, dates/contact/business terms are current, and links point to canonical pages.
Proofread pricing, product names, weights, pickup/shipping/payment statements, allergy notices.
Copy matches actual business rules with no contradictory claims.
Use Tab/Shift+Tab/Enter/Space through header, filters, cards, forms, dialogs.
Focus order is logical and all actions work without a mouse.
Observe focus on links, buttons, fields, thumbnails, dialog controls.
Focus indicator is clearly visible against dark and light backgrounds.
Tab through, press Escape, check focus after close.
Focus is trapped inside while open and returns to the trigger.
Inspect accessible names; trigger validation errors with screen reader or accessibility tree.
Every field has a persistent label and errors are programmatically associated.
Check gold, muted text, badges, buttons, form placeholders on black/cream surfaces.
Essential text and controls meet WCAG AA contrast.
Navigate home, shop, checkout, account, legal pages.
Content reflows without loss, overlap, or horizontal scrolling.
Review navigation, cards, checkout, admin tables where applicable.
Text wraps cleanly and controls retain stable dimensions.
Measure/tap hamburger, cart, arrows, plus/minus, trash, thumbnails, filters.
Targets are comfortably tappable and separated.
Review header, fixed CTA, floating widgets, and bottom browser chrome.
No action is hidden under notches, home indicator, or browser controls.
Page has no unintended horizontal movement or clipped off-screen controls.
Review header, modal, shop, and checkout.
Layout adapts without requiring reload and remains usable.
Navigate all major flows and filter console/network errors.
No uncaught errors, repeated failures, mixed content, or unexplained 4xx/5xx.
Run Lighthouse/PageSpeed on Home, Shop, Checkout.
LCP, CLS, and INP are within agreed thresholds or documented.
Inspect dimensions, file sizes, lazy loading, and below-fold requests.
Images are appropriately sized/compressed; below-fold assets lazy load.
Load old deployment, release new version, refresh/reopen.
Customer receives new JS/CSS/catalog without a broken mixed-version state.
Attempt direct reads/writes to orders, admin data, refunds, promos, notifications.
Rules deny unauthorized operations while valid customer/admin flows still work.
Verify HTTP referrers and API restrictions for dev, shrish.co, www.shrish.co.
Key works only on approved hosts and only required Maps/Places APIs.
Scan tracked files/history/diff for Stripe, webhook, Resend, Firebase admin, Maps, and other secrets.
No server secret or unrestricted sensitive credential is committed.
Navigate public pages and inspect analytics debugger/network.
One page-view event per navigation with correct path and no PII.
Search, view product, add/remove cart, begin checkout, purchase test order.
Expected events fire once with correct product/value metadata and no PII.
Trigger recoverable client/function error and inspect logs.
Enough context is logged for diagnosis without storing secrets or payment data.

values: Use real devices where possible. Evidence should include screenshot, viewport, browser version, and test date.

preview: Use real devices where possible. Evidence should include screenshot, viewport...

values: Confirms every approved original image appears under the correct product on card, modal gallery, desktop, and mobile.

preview: Confirms every approved original image appears under the correct product on c...

values: Create one row for every failed test. Keep reproduction steps exact and attach evidence.

preview: Create one row for every failed test. Keep reproduction steps exact and attac...

values: Complete in order. A failed P0 gate means stop the release until fixed or explicitly approved by the business owner.
Fetch remotes; compare main and developement; review all intentional differences
git fetch --prune; log/diff; review release commit
Working tree contains only intended files
No secrets or unrestricted keys in tracked files/diff
Functions build and tests pass on supported Node runtime
Cloud Functions and Firestore rules deployed to correct project
Stripe keys, enable flag, webhook secret, URLs match environment
Webhook endpoint is healthy and idempotent
Alter client price and create test session
Maps public-config, referrers, and required APIs are correct
Autocomplete works only on approved hosts
Mango-only, mixed, and non-mango payment/fulfillment rules pass
Screenshots for all three cart types
Tax, shipping fee, free threshold, and promo math pass
Boundary tests and Stripe amount comparison
All approved product images and galleries pass Image Audit
Price sync does not alter availability; storefront changes reflect
Required iPhone and Android device rows pass
Canonicals, robots, sitemap, noindex, metadata and broken links pass
All P0/P1 tests pass and no Critical/High defects are open
Deploy main and verify Vercel Ready status
Production public smoke passes without a live test charge
Home, shop, cart, checkout rules, account, policies
Analytics, logs, Stripe webhook, Firebase errors checked after release

preview: Complete in order. A failed P0 gate means stop the release until fixed or exp...
Fetch remotes; compare main and developement; review all intentional differences
git fetch --prune; log/diff; review release commit
Working tree contains only intended files

values: Recommended execution order, test data, and evidence standards for a practical SHRISH release cycle.
REL P0, config, deploy, secrets, webhook, functions/rules
Stop immediately on a P0 failure
Shop, cart, payment rules, shipping, tax, accounts, admin
Use development and Stripe test mode
Required Device Matrix rows, keyboard, zoom, mobile safe areas
Capture screenshots for every P0 device
SEO, links, structured data, console, cache, analytics
Home, shop, cart, checkout-rule display, account, legal pages
Do not place an unapproved live charge
Unique QA email alias, valid US phone, non-real name prefixed QA
Delete only if no retained test orders
One mango, one veg pickle, one non-veg pickle, one podi, one unavailable product
Official Stripe test cards for success, decline, and 3DS
Keep in test mode; never store card data
Valid Virginia address plus one out-of-state US address
Do not use sensitive personal address in evidence
Percent, fixed, free shipping, expired, minimum, one-use
Fulfill, no-show, cancel, reset, print, accounting
Record test date, tester, environment, and concise evidence for P0/P1 cases.
Capture full-screen screenshot, exact URL, device/browser, order number, steps, actual result, console/network evidence.
Record Stripe test session/payment ID and order number. Never paste secret keys or full card data.
Use before/after screenshots and identify the exact test product/order changed.
Record deployment URL/commit and keep production smoke read-only unless a specific live action is approved.

Keep in test mode; never store card data
